string.text: The MSBS Grot is a family of modular rifles developed and manufactured by FB Radom. Currently, the rifle is in service with the Polish Territorial Defence Forces and is scheduled to replace FB Beryl as the primary infantry weapon and service rifle of the Polish Armed Forces. This particular variant, designated C16A2 comes equipped with a 16-inch barrel and when compared to its older iterations: a longer handguard, reinforced firing pin, improved pistol grip and stock. It appears the previous owner replaced the factory stock with a Magpul CTR stock and also added a laser device and a handgrip.

The MSBS Grot is a family of modular rifles developed and manufactured by FB Radom. Currently, the rifle is in service with the Polish Territorial Defence Forces and is scheduled to replace FB Beryl as the primary infantry weapon and service rifle of the Polish Armed Forces. This particular variant, designated C10A2 comes equipped with a 10-inch barrel and shortened handguard to increase ergonomics as well as to decrease weight and its length for use in CQB environments at the cost of more recoil and slower bullet velocity.

The MSBS Grot is a family of modular rifles developed and manufactured by FB Radom. Currently, the rifle is in service with the Polish Territorial Defence Forces and is scheduled to replace FB Beryl as the primary infantry weapon and service rifle of the Polish Armed Forces. This particular variant, designated C10A2 comes equipped with a 10-inch barrel and shortened handguard to increase ergonomics as well as to decrease weight and its length for use in CQB environments at the cost of more recoil and slower bullet velocity.

The MSBS Grot is a family of modular rifles developed and manufactured by FB Radom. Currently, the rifle is in service with the Polish Territorial Defence Forces and is scheduled to replace FB Beryl as the primary infantry weapon and service rifle of the Polish Armed Forces. This particular variant, designated C16A2 comes equipped with a 16-inch barrel and when compared to its older iterations: a longer handguard, reinforced firing pin, improved pistol grip and stock. It appears the previous owner replaced the factory stock with a Magpul CTR stock and also added a laser device and a handgrip.
Ukrainian titanium made suppressor manufactured by the company STEEL.
